reasoning: The most recent known value (237,500) closely matches the temporary spike in initial claims seen around October 2024 due to hurricanes and labor strikes. Because the reference value is taken on October 26, the 4-week moving average at that time will fully incorporate these elevated single-week spikes. By the resolution date in January, these seasonal and one-off weather/strike anomalies will have washed out of the 4-week moving average window. Assuming the underlying economy does not enter a sharp recession during this 3-month period, mean reversion strongly suggests that the 4-week moving average will have decreased back to the trend level (approximately 210k-220k) rather than increased. Therefore, an increase relative to the locally elevated October 26 value is unlikely.
